## [00:00 - 03:04]

All right guys, in this video, I want to talk about getting into the mind of your avatar and you have this available to you. This is our customer persona canvas at the click, which is my company and we use this to get into things like demographics and vocation, things we talked about in the prior video, interests and hobbies and brands and values and we'll fill out an entire canvas like this for a customer. We'll give them a name up there and all of that stuff and you can absolutely 100% do that. But one of the things I want you to understand and that Molly has also going to be talking to you about is that demographics and vocation and all of those things, they aren't nearly as important as understanding the psychographics of the person that you're trying to reach. And in fact, it can be kind of dangerous to pigeonhole your customers into some demographic where you're using, for example, your ad targeting to not show ads to certain age groups or genders or people of certain professions when that demographic information is not actually helpful. There are times when it's helpful, but there are also a lot of times where we use demographics or we use other types of things that are not based on behavior, which is what we're going to get into in just a second, that is not helpful to your ad campaign to restrict people of any age or any gender or anything like that. Instead, what we all need to be doing is getting more into the psychographics of the person that we're trying to reach. And that's what this questionnaire is designed to do. It's called the customer avatar questionnaire. What I want you to do with this is answer these questions as thoroughly as you possibly can for your persona or your avatar, as we're calling them. And these are the kinds of questions that are going to lead to breakthrough type copy that you can use to sell products and services. So these are the questions. What is this person anxious about? And you might look at that and compare it to the persona that you're trying to reach, the person that you got in your head, and think that really doesn't apply. And that's okay. But spend a minute or two really thinking it through as to whether or not it does apply and that you can add some detail here about what this person is anxious about or what motivates this person.

## [03:04 - 06:09]

What is it that motivates this person to take action? Now, this doesn't have to, not yet anyway, necessarily tie back to the product or service that you sell. Right now, I just want you thinking about this person, this avatar, and what are they anxious about? What motivates them? And it may not be connected to your product. We will get there as we move through this module. Right now, I want you to thinking about what motivates them in general? What were they anxious about? Now, if it's connected to your product, that's great. And if it's not, that's okay. Put it down here as anyway. What about things that they remember? Is there something that you think this persona might remember fondly from their past? What does this person find complicated in their life? What is it that is complex in their life? What information do they find valuable? Some people find the news, for example, current events very valuable. Other people say, I don't even watch the news. I don't want to. Right? So are there sources or types of information that these people find valuable? Put that in here. How motivated is this person by money? And explain your answer. Right? So you're not just going to say a lot. You can say a lot because or not at all because or I would say moderately because and describe this person's motivation by money. How motivated is the person by health and wellness? Explain that. How motivated is the person by design and aesthetics? Do they care about how things look and how they function? Explain. What does this person find fun? What do they find entertaining? What do they do with their free time? What does this person find dangerous or risky in their life? What titles, positions, roles? What status are they proud of? Is there certain status that they're proud of? They don't want to lose that status. What do they want to aspire to in terms of a role position? So these are things they already have it obtained. They might be a mother or they might be a CEO or they might be whatever it is. What are these titles that they're proud of now and what is it that they're looking to get to? Are their titles, positions, roles that they want that they aspire to? Describe this person's average day. What are the things that they're doing during an average day? How motivated is this person by their outward appearance? Explain that. Now, when you go through these 14 questions, you're going to start to really get into the

## [06:09 - 06:44]

psychology of this person. And what we want you to do is not, don't tie this back to your product necessarily just yet. Put things down even that don't relate to your product. And as we move further through this module, you're going to start getting more and more connected to how does this person, this avatar that we have in mind here, what do they want and how does that connect back to my offer and what I'm selling?
